 {"id":"cass_memory_system-4zf","title":"[Utils] truncate - Text truncation with ellipsis","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:14.481959-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:14.481959-05:00","comments":[{"id":2,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-4zf","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function truncate(text: string, maxLength: number): string
```

## Implementation Details
- Check if text.length <= maxLength
- If yes, return text unchanged
- If no, return text.slice(0, maxLength - 3) + "..."
- The -3 accounts for ellipsis length
- Total output length will be exactly maxLength

## Why This Matters
Used for preview text in UI output. Keeps display clean and consistent. Example: truncate("This is a very long bullet content", 20) returns "This is a very lo..."

## Edge Cases to Handle
- maxLength < 3 - handle gracefully, maybe return just "..." or throw
- Empty string - return empty
- maxLength exactly text length - return unchanged
- Unicode characters - ensure slice doesn't break surrogate pairs
- Newlines in text - may want to replace with space first","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-52q","title":"[Cass] safeCassSearch - Search with automatic error recovery","description":"Implement safeCassSearch() wrapper with automatic error recovery.

## Signature
```typescript
async function safeCassSearch(
  query: string, 
  options: CassSearchOptions = {},
  cassPath = "cass"
): Promise<CassHit[]>
```

## Implementation Flow
1. Check cassAvailable() - return [] if cass not installed
2. Check cassNeedsIndex() - rebuild if needed
3. Call cassSearch() with provided options
4. Return results or [] on any failure

## Error Handling
- Never throws exceptions
- Always returns array (empty on error)
- Logs errors at appropriate level
- Performs automatic recovery:
  - Rebuilds index if missing
  - Reduces limit on timeout
  - Retries once after recovery

## Purpose
High-level wrapper for resilient search operations. Use this in:
- reflect command
- recall command  
- remind command
- Any user-facing search functionality

## Usage
```typescript
// Safe to use without try/catch
const hits = await safeCassSearch("error handling", { 
  limit: 10,
  days: 30 
});

if (hits.length === 0) {
  console.log("No results found");
} else {
  processHits(hits);
}
```

## vs cassSearch
- cassSearch: Low-level, may throw, caller handles errors
- safeCassSearch: High-level, never throws, auto-recovery","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:57.822142-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:48:07.889839-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5eh","title":"Phase 1: Foundation + High-ROI Core","description":"# EPIC: Foundation + High-ROI Core

## Purpose
Establish the core infrastructure and implement the highest-ROI features that provide immediate value. This phase creates the skeleton upon which all other features build.

## Background & Reasoning
The ACE (Agentic Context Engineering) framework emphasizes three key innovations:
1. **Generator** - Pre-task context hydration
2. **Reflector** - LLM-powered insight extraction  
3. **Curator** - Deterministic delta merging (NO LLM rewriting)

This phase implements the Generator (context command) and foundational types needed for all subsequent phases.

## Priority Features (from ROI analysis)
- P1: Confidence decay + event timestamps (20× impact)
- P2: Structured JSON context output (10× impact)
- P4: Effective score (4× harmful multiplier) (5× impact)
- P10: State lifecycle draft/active/retired (10× impact)
- P12: Secret sanitization (∞ security-critical)
- P14: Kind enum categorization (3× impact)

## Key Deliverables
1. `cass-memory init` - Initialize configuration and playbook
2. `cass-memory context --json` - Pre-flight task hydration
3. Complete type system with all enhancements
4. cass integration wrapper with error handling
5. Secret sanitization pipeline

## Architecture Decisions
- Use Bun for all JS/TS operations (per AGENTS.md rules)
- Vercel AI SDK for LLM provider abstraction
- Zod for runtime type validation and LLM structured output
- YAML for playbook storage (human-readable, git-friendly)

## Success Criteria
- An agent can run `cass-memory context "task"` and receive relevant bullets
- Bullets have proper decay-adjusted scores
- No secrets leak into stored diary entries or playbook
- Configuration cascades from global to repo-level

## Dependencies
None - this is the foundation phase","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:16:01.370891-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:16:01.370891-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.1","title":"Project Setup: Bun + TypeScript + Dependencies","description":"# Feature: Project Setup

## Purpose
Initialize the project with Bun, TypeScript, and all required dependencies.

## Technical Decisions
- **Runtime**: Bun (per AGENTS.md: "Use bun for everything JS/TS")
- **LLM SDK**: Vercel AI SDK (supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Google)
- **Validation**: Zod (structured LLM output, runtime type safety)
- **YAML**: yaml package (for playbook read/write)
- **Output**: chalk (terminal colors for human-readable output)

## Files to Create
1. `package.json` - Dependencies, scripts, bin entry
2. `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript configuration
3. `src/` directory structure

## Package.json Specifics
```json
{
  "name": "cass-memory",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "type": "module",
  "bin": { "cass-memory": "./dist/cass-memory.js" },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "bun run src/cass-memory.ts",
    "build": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --outfile dist/cass-memory",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "bun test"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "ai": "^4.0.0",
    "@ai-sdk/openai": "^1.0.0",
    "@ai-sdk/anthropic": "^1.0.0", 
    "@ai-sdk/google": "^1.0.0",
    "zod": "^3.23.0",
    "yaml": "^2.3.0",
    "chalk": "^5.3.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/bun": "latest",
    "typescript": "^5.0.0"
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- `bun install` succeeds
- `bun run dev --help` shows CLI help
- `bun run typecheck` passes
- Directory structure matches plan

## No Dependencies

## Exact File Content
```json
{
  "name": "cass-memory",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "type": "module",
  "description": "Agent-agnostic reflection and memory system implementing ACE framework",
  "author": "Your Name",
  "license": "MIT",
  "bin": {
    "cass-memory": "./dist/cass-memory.js"
  },
  "main": "./dist/cass-memory.js",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "bun run src/cass-memory.ts",
    "build": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --outfile dist/cass-memory",
    "build:linux": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=bun-linux-x64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-linux-x64",
    "build:macos-arm": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=bun-darwin-arm64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-darwin-arm64",
    "build:macos-x64": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=bun-darwin-x64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-darwin-x64",
    "build:windows": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=bun-windows-x64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-windows-x64.exe",
    "build:all": "bun run build:linux && bun run build:macos-arm && bun run build:macos-x64 && bun run build:windows",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "bun test",
    "test:watch": "bun test --watch"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "ai": "^4.0.0",
    "@ai-sdk/openai": "^1.0.0",
    "@ai-sdk/anthropic": "^1.0.0",
    "@ai-sdk/google": "^1.0.0",
    "zod": "^3.23.0",
    "yaml": "^2.3.0",
    "chalk": "^5.3.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/bun": "latest",
    "typescript": "^5.0.0"
  },
  "engines": {
    "bun": ">=1.0.0"
  }
}
```

## Why These Dependencies
- **ai**: Vercel AI SDK - unified interface for LLM providers
- **@ai-sdk/openai**: OpenAI provider (GPT-4, GPT-3.5)
- **@ai-sdk/anthropic**: Anthropic provider (Claude models)
- **@ai-sdk/google**: Google provider (Gemini models)
- **zod**: Runtime type validation + LLM structured output
- **yaml**: Parse/write YAML playbooks (human-readable, git-friendly)
- **chalk**: Terminal colors for human-readable output

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] File created at project root
- [ ] `bun install` completes without errors
- [ ] All dependencies resolve correctly
- [ ] `bun run dev --help` executes (even if shows placeholder)","notes":"package.json added per spec; bun install succeeded; bun run dev --help works.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:32:59.788546-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:48:03.188564-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:42:22.482223-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.1.1","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:32:59.789618-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.1.2","title":"[Setup] Create tsconfig.json for strict TypeScript","description":"# Task: Create tsconfig.json

## Exact File Content
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ESNext",
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "lib": ["ESNext"],
    "types": ["bun-types"],
    "strict": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "verbatimModuleSyntax": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true,
    "exactOptionalPropertyTypes": true,
    "noImplicitReturns": true,
    "noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true
  },
  "include": ["src/**/*.ts"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
}
```

## Why These Options
- **strict: true**: Enables all strict type checking
- **noUnusedLocals/Parameters**: Catch dead code
- **noUncheckedIndexedAccess**: Force null checks on array access
- **exactOptionalPropertyTypes**: Stricter optional property handling
- **verbatimModuleSyntax**: Modern ESM handling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] File created at project root
- [ ] `bun run typecheck` passes with no errors
- [ ] Strict mode catches type errors properly","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:33:01.022879-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:41:03.843066-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:40:40.297645-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.1.2","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:33:01.02374-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.1.3","title":"[Setup] Create src/ directory structure","description":"# Task: Create src/ Directory Structure

## Directory Layout
```
src/
├── cass-memory.ts    # Main CLI entry point
├── types.ts          # All TypeScript interfaces + Zod schemas
├── utils.ts          # Shared utility functions
├── config.ts         # Configuration loading/saving
├── cass.ts           # cass CLI wrapper
├── sanitize.ts       # Secret sanitization
├── scoring.ts        # Decay + effective score algorithms
├── playbook.ts       # Playbook read/write/merge
├── llm.ts            # LLM provider abstraction
├── diary.ts          # Diary generation
├── reflect.ts        # Reflector pipeline
├── validate.ts       # Scientific validation
├── curate.ts         # Deterministic delta application
└── commands/         # Command implementations
    ├── init.ts
    ├── context.ts
    ├── diary.ts
    ├── reflect.ts
    ├── mark.ts
    ├── forget.ts
    ├── doctor.ts
    ├── stats.ts
    ├── validate.ts
    ├── audit.ts
    └── project.ts
```

## File Stubs
Each file should start with a minimal stub:
```typescript

export {}; // Placeholder

// src/utils.ts
export {}; // Placeholder
// etc.
```

-{"id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.10","title":"CLI Scaffolding: src/cass-memory.ts","description":"# Feature: CLI Scaffolding (src/cass-memory.ts)

## Purpose
Main CLI entry point with command parsing, help text, and routing to
command handlers.

## CLI Structure
```
cass-memory <command> [options]

Commands:
  init              Initialize configuration and playbook
  context <task>    Get relevant context for a task (Phase 1)
  diary <path>      Generate structured diary from a session (Phase 2)
  reflect           Run reflection cycle on recent sessions (Phase 2)
  validate <rule>   Validate proposed rule against history (Phase 3)
  curate            Apply deltas to playbook (Phase 2)
  mark <id>         Record bullet usage (helpful/harmful) (Phase 2)
  forget <id>       Permanently block a pattern (Phase 2)
  audit             Check sessions for rule violations (Phase 4)
  playbook          Manage playbook entries (Phase 2)
  project           Export playbook to AGENTS.md (Phase 4)
  stats             Show playbook health metrics (Phase 3)
  doctor            Check system health (Phase 2)

Global Options:
  --json            Output in JSON format
  --verbose         Enable verbose logging
  --config <path>   Use custom config file
  --help            Show help
  --version         Show version
```

## Implementation Approach
Use simple argument parsing (no heavy dependencies):
```typescript
const args = process.argv.slice(2);
const command = args[0];
const flags = parseFlags(args.slice(1));

switch (command) {
  case "init": return initCommand(flags);
  case "context": return contextCommand(args[1], flags);
  // ...
  default: showHelp();
}
```

## Global Flag Handling
```typescript
interface GlobalFlags {
  json: boolean;
  verbose: boolean;
  config?: string;
  help: boolean;
  version: boolean;
}

function parseFlags(args: string[]): GlobalFlags & Record<string, any>;
```

## Output Formatting
```typescript
// JSON output (for agents)
function outputJson(data: any): void {
  console.log(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2));
}

// Human output (for developers)
function outputHuman(data: any, formatter: (d: any) => string): void {
  console.log(formatter(data));
}

// Auto-select based on --json flag
function output(data: any, formatter?: (d: any) => string, flags?: GlobalFlags): void {
  if (flags?.json) outputJson(data);
  else if (formatter) outputHuman(data, formatter);
  else console.log(data);
}
```

## Error Handling
- Exit code 0: Success
- Exit code 1: User error (bad input, missing file)
- Exit code 2: System error (cass unavailable, API error)
- Always output errors to stderr","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:19:51.077755-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:19:51.077755-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.10","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:19:51.078389-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.10","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:20:12.453245-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.10","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:20:12.53561-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.11","title":"Init Command: cass-memory init","description":"# Feature: Init Command

## Purpose
Initialize cass-memory for first-time users:
1. Create directory structure
2. Create default config
3. Create empty playbook
4. Verify cass availability
5. Offer to run initial index

## Usage
```bash
cass-memory init [--force] [--json]
```

## Behavior
```typescript
async function initCommand(flags: InitFlags): Promise<void> {
  // 1. Check if already initialized
  const configExists = await fileExists("~/.cass-memory/config.json");
  if (configExists && !flags.force) {
    error("Already initialized. Use --force to reinitialize.");
    return;
  }
  
  // 2. Create directories
  await ensureDir("~/.cass-memory/diary");
  await ensureDir("~/.cass-memory/reflections");
  await ensureDir("~/.cass-memory/embeddings");
  await ensureDir("~/.cass-memory/cost");
  
  // 3. Create default config
  const config = getDefaultConfig();
  await saveConfig(config);
  
  // 4. Create empty playbook
  const playbook = createEmptyPlaybook("global");
  await savePlaybook(playbook, config.playbookPath);
  
  // 5. Check cass availability
  const cassOk = cassAvailable(config.cassPath);
  if (!cassOk) {
    warn("cass is not available. Some features will not work.");
    warn("Install cass: https://github.com/your/cass");
  }
  
  // 6. Output result
  output({
    success: true,
    configPath: expandPath("~/.cass-memory/config.json"),
    playbookPath: expandPath(config.playbookPath),
    cassAvailable: cassOk
  }, formatInitResult, flags);
}
```

## Directory Structure Created
```
~/.cass-memory/
├── config.json           # Tool configuration
├── playbook.yaml         # Global playbook (empty)
├── diary/                # Diary entries (empty)
├── reflections/          # Reflection logs
├── embeddings/           # Embedding cache (Phase 3)
└── cost/                 # LLM cost tracking
```

### JSON (--json)

{
  "success": true,
  "configPath": "/Users/x/.cass-memory/config.json",
  "playbookPath": "/Users/x/.cass-memory/playbook.yaml",
  "cassAvailable": true
}
```

### Human-readable
```
✓ Created ~/.cass-memory/config.json
✓ Created ~/.cass-memory/playbook.yaml
✓ Created directories: diary/, reflections/, embeddings/
✓ cass available: yes

cass-memory initialized successfully!

Next steps:
  cass-memory context "your task" --json  # Get context for a task
  cass-memory doctor                       # Check system health

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.12","title":"Context Command: cass-memory context (P2 - Generator)","description":"# Feature: Context Command (The Generator)

## Purpose
The "Generator" phase of ACE: retrieve relevant context BEFORE starting a task.
This is the primary interface for agents to access accumulated knowledge.

## Usage
```bash
cass-memory context "Fix authentication timeout bug" --json
cass-memory context "Add dark mode support" --top 10 --days 30
```

## Process Flow
```
1. Parse task description
2. Generate search keywords (LLM or heuristic)
3. Score playbook bullets by relevance
4. Query cass for relevant history
5. Check deprecated patterns for warnings
6. Format output (JSON or human)
```

## Implementation
```typescript
async function contextCommand(task: string, flags: ContextFlags): Promise<void> {
  const config = await loadConfig();
  const playbook = await loadMergedPlaybook(config);
  
  // 1. Extract keywords from task
  const keywords = extractKeywords(task);
  
  // 2. Score and filter bullets
  const scoredBullets = playbook.bullets
    .filter(b => !b.deprecated && b.state !== "retired")
    .map(b => ({
      ...b,
      relevanceScore: scoreBulletRelevance(b.content, b.tags, keywords),
      effectiveScore: getEffectiveScore(b, config)
    }))
    .filter(b => b.relevanceScore > 0)
    .sort((a, b) => (b.relevanceScore * b.effectiveScore) - (a.relevanceScore * a.effectiveScore))
    .slice(0, flags.top || config.maxBulletsInContext);
  
  // 3. Query cass for history
  const cassHits = await safeCassSearch(keywords.join(" "), {
    limit: flags.history || config.maxHistoryInContext,
    days: flags.days || config.sessionLookbackDays
  }, config.cassPath);
  
  // 4. Check deprecated patterns
  const warnings = checkDeprecatedPatterns(task, playbook.deprecatedPatterns);
  
  // 5. Format output
  const result: ContextResult = {
    task,
    bullets: scoredBullets.map(b => ({
      id: b.id,
      content: b.content,
      category: b.category,
      kind: b.kind,
      relevanceScore: b.relevanceScore,
      effectiveScore: b.effectiveScore
    })),
    history: cassHits,
    warnings,
    formattedPrompt: formatContextPrompt(scoredBullets, cassHits, warnings)
  };
  
  output(result, formatContextHuman, flags);
}
```

## JSON Output Format (P2)
```json
{
  "task": "Fix authentication timeout bug",
  "bullets": [
    {
      "id": "b-abc123",
      "content": "For auth timeout issues, check session TTL in config...",
      "category": "debugging",
      "kind": "workflow_rule",
      "relevanceScore": 8.5,
      "effectiveScore": 12.3
    }
  ],
  "history": [
    {
      "source_path": "~/.claude/sessions/abc.jsonl",
      "line_number": 142,
      "agent": "claude",
      "snippet": "Fixed auth timeout by increasing session TTL...",
      "score": 0.85
    }
  ],
  "warnings": [
    "Deprecated: AuthHandler - use AuthService from @/lib/auth"
  ],
  "formattedPrompt": "## PLAYBOOK CONTEXT\n..."
}
```

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.2","title":"Type System: Complete TypeScript Interfaces + Zod Schemas","description":"# Feature: Type System (src/types.ts)

## Purpose
Define all TypeScript interfaces and Zod schemas that form the contract for
the entire system. This is the most critical file - everything depends on it.

## Key Data Models

### PlaybookBullet (Enhanced)
```typescript
interface PlaybookBullet {
  // Identity
  id: string;                    // "b-{timestamp36}-{random}"
  
  // Scope
  scope: "global" | "workspace";
  workspace?: string;
  
  // Content
  category: string;
  content: string;               // 10-500 chars
  searchPointer?: string;        // Optional cass query
  
  // Kind (NEW - P14)
  kind: "project_convention" | "stack_pattern" | "workflow_rule" | "anti_pattern";
  
  // State Lifecycle (NEW - P10)
  state: "draft" | "active" | "retired";
  
  // Tracking with decay (NEW - P1)
  feedbackEvents: FeedbackEvent[];  // Timestamped events
  
  // Legacy counts (computed from feedbackEvents)
  helpfulCount: number;
  harmfulCount: number;
  
  // Lifecycle timestamps
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
  deprecated: boolean;
  replacedBy?: string;
  deprecationReason?: string;
  
  // Provenance
  sourceSessions: string[];
  sourceAgents: string[];
  
  // Tagging
  tags: string[];
  
  // Computed (optional, cached)
  embedding?: number[];
}
```

### FeedbackEvent (NEW - P1)
```typescript
interface FeedbackEvent {
  type: "helpful" | "harmful";
  timestamp: string;              // ISO 8601
  sessionPath?: string;
  reason?: string;                // For harmful
  decayedValue?: number;          // Cached after decay calculation
}
```

### DiaryEntry
- id, sessionPath
- timestamp, agent, workspace, duration
- status: success | failure | mixed
- accomplishments, decisions, challenges, preferences, keyLearnings
- relatedSessions (cross-agent enrichment)
- tags, searchAnchors

### Delta Types
- add, helpful, harmful, replace, deprecate, merge

### Configuration
- Provider settings (openai/anthropic/google)
- Path settings (playbookPath, diaryDir)
- Thresholds (dedup, prune, maxIterations)
- Feature flags

## Zod Schemas
All interfaces must have corresponding Zod schemas for:
1. Runtime validation
2. LLM structured output (generateObject)
3. YAML parsing validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- All types from plan implemented
- Zod schemas match interfaces
- Export all types for use across modules
- TSDoc comments on complex types","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:17:16.768077-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:48:03.190304-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:43:51.864498-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.2","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:17:16.768997-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.2","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:20:11.467488-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-a4g","title":"[Utils] formatRelativeTime - Human-readable relative time","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:11.043941-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:11.043941-05:00","comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-a4g","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function formatRelativeTime(isoDate: string): string
```

## Implementation Details
- Parse isoDate to Date object
- Calculate difference from now in milliseconds
- Convert to days: Math.floor(diff / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24))
- Return string based on days:
  - 0 days: "today"
  - 1 day: "yesterday"
  - 2-6 days: "N days ago"
  - 7-29 days: "N weeks ago" (divide by 7, round)
  - 30+ days: "N months ago" (divide by 30, round)

## Why This Matters
Used in human-readable output (not JSON mode). Makes timestamps easier to understand at a glance. "2 days ago" is clearer than "2024-01-13T14:30:00.000Z".

## Edge Cases to Handle
- Future dates - return "in the future" or handle as 0 days
- Invalid ISO string - throw error
- Very old dates - "N months ago" can get large, consider "N years ago" for 365+ days","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-alcn","title":"[CLI:reflect] updateProcessedLog - Track processed sessions","description":"Record processed sessions to prevent reprocessing.

Function signature:
```typescript
async function updateProcessedLog(
  log: ProcessedLog,
  entries: ProcessedEntry[]
): Promise<void>
```

Implementation:
1. Load existing processed.log:
   - File location: ${memoryDir}/processed.log
   - Tab-delimited format
   - Create if doesn't exist

2. Add new entries:
   - Each entry represents one processed session
   - Format: {id}\t{timestamp}\t{sessionPath}\t{deltasProposed}\t{deltasApplied}\n
   - Example: sess-abc123\t2025-01-15T10:30:00Z\t/Users/x/.claude/sessions/001.jsonl\t5\t3

3. Write to disk:
   - Append mode (don't overwrite)
   - Atomic write (temp file + rename)
   - Ensure proper permissions

ProcessedEntry type:
```typescript
interface ProcessedEntry {
  id: string;              // session ID
  timestamp: string;       // ISO 8601
  sessionPath: string;     // absolute path
  deltasProposed: number;  // from reflector
  deltasApplied: number;   // from curator
}
```

ProcessedLog class:
```typescript
class ProcessedLog {
  private entries: Map<string, ProcessedEntry>;
  
  load(): Promise<void>
  has(sessionPath: string): boolean
  add(entry: ProcessedEntry): void
  save(): Promise<void>
}
```

The log prevents duplicate processing and provides audit trail.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:50:18.282321-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:50:18.282321-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ank","title":"[Diary] loadDiaryEntry - Read from disk","description":"Read and validate diary entry from disk.

async function loadDiaryEntry(diaryPath: string): Promise<DiaryEntry>

1. Verify file exists at diaryPath
2. Read file contents as UTF-8 string
3. Parse JSON string to object
4. Validate against DiaryEntrySchema using Zod
5. Return validated DiaryEntry

Validation Features:
- Type checking for all fields
- Required field validation
- Date format validation for timestamp
- Array content validation
- Status enum validation (success/failure/mixed)

Error Handling:
- FileNotFoundError if path doesn't exist
- JSONParseError if invalid JSON
- SchemaValidationError if missing/invalid fields
- Detailed error messages for debugging

Schema Migration:
- If old diary version detected, apply migration
- Add default values for new optional fields
- Preserve backward compatibility

Key Features:
- Full schema validation ensures type safety
- Detailed validation errors for debugging
- Migration support for schema evolution
- UTF-8 encoding handling

Use Cases:
- Loading diary for evidence correlation
- Reading diaries for stats computation
- Fetching diary for display in UI
- Validating diary integrity

Output:
Returns fully validated DiaryEntry object ready for use.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:09.388329-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:18.411032-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-apt","title":"[Cass] cassSearch - Execute cass search with options","description":"Implement cassSearch() to execute cass search with comprehensive options.

## Signature
```typescript
interface CassSearchOptions {
  limit?: number;
  days?: number;
  agent?: string;
  workspace?: string;
  fields?: string;
  maxTokens?: number;
  highlight?: boolean;
  timeout?: number;
}

interface CassHit {
  sessionPath: string;
  lineNumber: number;
  content: string;
  timestamp?: string;
  score?: number;
}

async function cassSearch(
  query: string, 
  options: CassSearchOptions = {},
  cassPath = "cass"
): Promise<CassHit[]>
```

## CLI Command
`cass search "{query}" --robot [--limit N] [--days N] [--agent A] [--workspace W] [--fields F] [--max-tokens N] [--highlight] [--timeout N]`

## Implementation
- Build command with all provided options
- Use execFile() with maxBuffer: 50MB for large results
- Parse JSON output from --robot flag
- Return array of CassHit objects

## Error Handling
Check exit code and handle intelligently:
- Exit 0: Success, parse and return hits
- Exit 3: Index missing - rebuild index, then retry once
- Exit 10: Timeout - reduce limit by 50%, retry once
- Exit 4: Not found - return empty array
- Other errors: Log and return empty array

## Options Details
- limit: Max hits to return (default: 10)
- days: Only search last N days
- agent: Filter by agent name (e.g., "claude-code")
- workspace: Filter by workspace path
- fields: Comma-separated fields to search
- maxTokens: Truncate results to N tokens
- highlight: Include highlighting markers in results
- timeout: Command timeout in seconds

## Usage
```typescript
const hits = await cassSearch("bug fix", { 
  limit: 20, 
  days: 7, 
  agent: "claude-code" 
});
```","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:57.153732-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:48:07.014803-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ar2","title":"[Curator] invertToAntiPattern - Convert harmful rule to pitfall","description":"Convert a harmful bullet into an anti-pattern (learning from mistakes).

Signature:
function invertToAntiPattern(bullet: PlaybookBullet): PlaybookBullet

KEY INNOVATION: Instead of deleting harmful rules, INVERT them to preserve learning.

Process:
1. Create new bullet with inverted semantics:
   - content: Prefix with 'AVOID: ' or 'DON\'T: '
   - Example: 'Use any type' → 'AVOID: Using any type without explicit reason'
   
2. Set bullet fields:
   - type: 'anti-pattern'
   - kind: 'anti_pattern'
   - category: same as original
   - maturity: 'candidate' (starts fresh)
   - state: 'active'
   
3. Preserve provenance:
   - source: copy from original
   - originalId: link back to harmful bullet
   - createdAt: current timestamp
   - derivedFrom: original bullet ID
   
4. Reset feedback (fresh start):
   - helpfulCount: 0
   - harmfulCount: 0
   - helpfulEvents: []
   - harmfulEvents: []
   
5. Return new anti-pattern bullet

Why inversion matters:
- Preserves WHAT NOT TO DO
- Prevents future developers from making same mistake
- Converts negative experience into positive guidance
- Maintains complete learning history

Example transformation:
Original (harmful): 'Always use any type for flexibility'
Anti-pattern: 'AVOID: Using any type without explicit reason. Prefer specific types for type safety.'

The anti-pattern must earn trust like any other candidate bullet.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:04.707658-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:47.213445-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-b5q","title":"[Security] compileExtraPatterns - User pattern compilation","description":"Implement compileExtraPatterns() to convert user pattern strings to RegExp objects.

Function signature:
```typescript
function compileExtraPatterns(patterns: string[]): RegExp[]
```

Implementation requirements:
1. Parse string patterns into RegExp objects
2. Add 'gi' flags automatically (global, case-insensitive)
3. Catch and warn on invalid regex syntax
4. Return only valid patterns (skip invalid ones)

Error handling:
- Log warning for invalid patterns: "[sanitize] Invalid regex pattern: <pattern>"
- Continue processing remaining patterns
- Never throw - return partial results

Example:
```typescript
const patterns = [
  "INTERNAL_[A-Z_]+_KEY",
  "[invalid(regex",  // syntax error
  "MY_SECRET_\\w+"
];
const compiled = compileExtraPatterns(patterns);
// Returns: [/INTERNAL_[A-Z_]+_KEY/gi, /MY_SECRET_\w+/gi]
// Logs: "[sanitize] Invalid regex pattern: [invalid(regex"
```

Called at startup to pre-compile user patterns from config.

Priority: 0 (Security Critical - P12)","notes":"Implemented compileExtraPatterns in src/sanitize.ts with gi flags, error handling, and warnings","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:51.253402-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:53:18.249516-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:52:23.874542-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-b96","title":"[Generator] generateSuggestedQueries - Follow-up search suggestions","description":"Generate human-readable cass search suggestions for follow-up investigation.

SIGNATURE:
function generateSuggestedQueries(task: string, keywords: string[]): string[]

PURPOSE:
Provide ready-to-run cass commands that the user can execute to dig deeper if the provided context isn't sufficient. Helps with iterative investigation.

OUTPUT:
Array of 3-5 formatted cass command strings

FORMAT:
'cass search "{query}" --days {days} [--agent {agent}]'

EXAMPLE:
Task: 'Fix authentication timeout bug'
Keywords: ['authentication', 'timeout', 'token', 'session', 'jwt']

Output: [
  'cass search "authentication timeout" --days 30',
  'cass search "token refresh" --days 60',
  'cass search "session expiry" --agent claude',
  'cass search "jwt validation error" --days 90'
]

QUERY GENERATION STRATEGY:
1. Use top 2-3 keyword combinations from keywords array
2. Add task-derived queries (extract noun phrases)
3. Vary --days parameter (30, 60, 90) for different time scopes
4. Include --agent filter if config.preferredAgent is set
5. Prioritize error/problem-oriented queries ('error', 'fix', 'bug')

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Escape quotes in query strings
- Limit to 5 suggestions (avoid overwhelming)
- Include at least one broad query (first keyword only)
- Include at least one specific query (multi-keyword phrase)

USE CASE:
User receives context but wants to explore more:
'The context you provided is helpful, but I want to see more examples of token refresh handling.'
→ They can run: cass search "token refresh" --days 60

INTEGRATION:
These queries appear at the end of markdown output under '### Dig Deeper' section
Also included in JSON output for programmatic access

ALTERNATIVE NAMES:
- 'Suggested Searches'
- 'Follow-up Queries'
- 'Explore More'","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:00.569423-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:24.227276-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-v625","title":"[Tracking] Processed log file format","description":"File location: ~/.cass-memory/reflections/global.processed.log

Format: Tab-separated values (TSV) with header comment

Example file:
```
# cass-memory processed sessions log
diary-abc123	~/.claude/projects/app/session-001.jsonl	2025-12-07T10:30:00Z	5	3
diary-def456	~/.cursor/state.vscdb:session-xyz	2025-12-07T11:00:00Z	8	6
diary-ghi789	/workspace/.aider.input.history	2025-12-07T12:15:00Z	3	3
```

Columns (tab-separated):
1. diaryId: Unique ID for diary entry (or entry ID if no diary)
2. sessionPath: Full path to original session
3. processedAt: ISO 8601 timestamp
4. deltasProposed: Count of proposed deltas
5. deltasApplied: Count of applied deltas

Notes:
- TSV for easy grep/awk processing

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-v6bn","title":"[Semantic] getEmbedder - Lazy-load embedding pipeline","description":"Singleton pattern for embedding pipeline - model loaded once per process.

Implementation:
```typescript
import { pipeline } from "@xenova/transformers";

let embedder: any = null;

async function getEmbedder(): Promise<any> {
  if (!embedder) {
    // First call downloads model (~23MB) if not cached
    embedder = await pipeline(
      "feature-extraction",
      "Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2"
    );
  }
  return embedder;
}
```

Performance:
- First call: ~500ms (model loading)
- Subsequent calls: <1ms (return cached instance)
- Model cached to ~/.cache/huggingface/

Error handling:
- Network errors during download
- Model incompatibility
- Fallback to keyword-only mode if fails

Module scope:
- Keep embedder at module level
- Never re-instantiate
- Thread-safe (single-threaded Node.js)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:28.018624-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:57.43482-05:00","comments":[{"id":32,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-v6bn","author":"jemanuel","text":"Singleton pattern for embedding pipeline - model loaded once per process.

Implementation:
```typescript
import { pipeline } from "@xenova/transformers";

let embedder: any = null;

async function getEmbedder(): Promise<any> {
  if (!embedder) {
    // First call downloads model (~23MB) if not cached
    embedder = await pipeline(
      "feature-extraction",
      "Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2"
    );
  }
  return embedder;
}
```

Performance:
- First call: ~500ms (model loading)
- Subsequent calls: <1ms (return cached instance)
- Model cached to ~/.cache/huggingface/

Error handling:
- Network errors during download
- Model incompatibility
- Fallback to keyword-only mode if fails

Module scope:
- Keep embedder at module level
- Never re-instantiate
- Thread-safe (single-threaded Node.js)","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-vh56","title":"[Docs] CHANGELOG.md format and maintenance","description":"Create and maintain CHANGELOG.md following Keep a Changelog format.

Format:
## [Unreleased]

## [0.1.0] - 2025-12-07

### Added
- Initial release of cass-memory
- Commands: init, context, diary, reflect, validate, mark, audit, playbook, project
- Advanced commands: forget, stats, doctor, top, stale, why, similar
- Confidence decay algorithm with configurable half-life
- Scientific validation of playbook integrity
- Three-layer memory model (Diary → Playbook → Project)
- ACE pipeline (Generator → Reflector → Validator → Curator)
- JSON output support for all commands
- Cross-platform binary compilation (macOS, Linux, Windows)

### Changed
### Deprecated
### Removed
### Fixed
### Security

Maintain changelog with every release and keep Unreleased section for ongoing work","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:41.521876-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:58.010652-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-vol","title":"[Types] HarmfulReason - Structured reasons for harmful marks","description":"## Overview
Define enumeration of structured reasons for marking a bullet as 'harmful'. Enables analysis of WHY rules fail, not just THAT they fail.

## Purpose
When a playbook rule causes problems, capturing the specific failure mode enables:
1. Pattern detection - Identify systemic issues (e.g., 'outdated rules are most common problem')
2. Targeted improvements - Address root causes (e.g., add validation for outdated patterns)
3. Better refinement - Reflector can learn from failure categories
4. User insights - Help users understand what went wrong

## Enumeration Values

### caused_bug
- Definition: Following the rule directly introduced a defect or error
- Example: 'Use AuthService.login()' but AuthService was refactored to AuthManager
- Severity: HIGH - broke functionality
- Action: Often requires immediate deprecation or replacement

### wasted_time
- Definition: Advice led to unnecessary work or dead ends
- Example: 'Run full test suite before commit' when project has no tests
- Severity: MEDIUM - productivity loss
- Action: Refine rule to check preconditions

### contradicted_requirements
- Definition: Rule conflicted with actual user needs or project goals
- Example: 'Use REST API' when project mandate is GraphQL
- Severity: MEDIUM - misalignment
- Action: Update rule or mark as wrong scope

### wrong_context
- Definition: Rule is valid but was applied in inappropriate situation
- Example: 'Use React hooks' suggested in Vue.js project
- Severity: LOW - scope issue
- Action: Improve scope filtering (workspace/framework)

### outdated
- Definition: Rule was correct historically but no longer applicable
- Example: 'Use class components' after project migrated to hooks
- Severity: MEDIUM - temporal drift
- Action: Add verification check or automatic expiration

### other
- Definition: Catchall for unclassified failures
- Example: Edge cases not fitting above categories
- Severity: VARIABLE
- Action: Review and potentially add new category

## Type Definition
type HarmfulReason = 'caused_bug' | 'wasted_time' | 'contradicted_requirements' | 'wrong_context' | 'outdated' | 'other'

## Usage in FeedbackEvent
const harmfulEvent: FeedbackEvent = { timestamp: '2025-12-07T14:30:00Z', sessionPath: '/path/to/session.jsonl', reason: 'caused_bug', context: 'AuthService was refactored to AuthManager' }

## Analytics Use Cases
- Stats command: Show distribution of failure reasons
- Audit command: Group violations by reason
- Reflector: Learn patterns (e.g., 'outdated' rules need verification)
- Auto-prune: Higher weight for 'caused_bug' vs 'wrong_context'

## Acceptance Criteria
- TypeScript type with 6 enum values
- JSDoc for each value explaining semantics
- Examples of each failure mode in docs
- Integration with FeedbackEvent type
- Used in harmful event creation flows","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:57.133573-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:50:18.03124-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-vuxg","title":"[Config] detectRepoContext - Find .cass/ directory","description":"Function signature:
```typescript
async function detectRepoContext(): Promise<{
  inRepo: boolean;
  repoRoot?: string;
  cassDir?: string;
}>
```

Implementation:
1. Run `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` to find repo root
   - If fails: not in a git repo, return { inRepo: false }
2. Check if .cass/ directory exists in repo root
3. Return context information

Return values:
- inRepo: true if in a git repository
- repoRoot: absolute path to git repo root (if inRepo)
- cassDir: absolute path to .cass/ directory (if exists)

Used by config loader to determine if repo-level config should be loaded.
Called at startup of most commands to establish context.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:54.839482-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:54.839482-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-w4g","title":"[Utils] extractKeywords - Stop word filtered keyword extraction","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:13.74917-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:13.74917-05:00","comments":[{"id":33,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-w4g","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function extractKeywords(text: string): string[]
```

## Implementation Details
- Tokenize text using tokenize() helper
- Define stop words list (~80 common words):
  - Articles: the, a, an
  - Verbs: is, are, was, were, be, been, have, has, had, do, does, did, will, would, could, should, can
  - Prepositions: to, of, in, for, on, with, at, by, from, as, into, through, during, before, after
  - Pronouns: I, you, he, she, it, we, they, me, him, her, us, them
  - Conjunctions: and, or, but, if, when, where, why, how
  - Other: this, that, these, those, what, which, who, there, here
- Filter out stop words: tokens.filter(t => !stopWords.includes(t))
- Return top 10 keywords by frequency (or all if fewer than 10)

## Why This Matters
Used for bullet scoring and cass query generation. Removing stop words focuses on meaningful terms. "Fix authentication bug" extracts ["fix", "authentication", "bug"] not ["fix", "the", "authentication", "bug"].

## Edge Cases to Handle
- All stop words - return []
- No stop words - return all tokens (up to 10)
- Fewer than 10 keywords - return all
- Duplicate keywords - count frequency, return unique tokens","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-w5xt","title":"[Diary] formatRawSession - Parse raw session formats","description":"Format raw session files when cass export is unavailable.

Function Signature:
function formatRawSession(content: string, ext: string): string

Supported Extensions and Formats:

1. Markdown (.md):
   - Return content as-is
   - Already human-readable
   - Used by aider

2. JSONL (.jsonl):
   - Parse each line as separate JSON object
   - Expected schema: { role: string, content: string }
   - Format as: "**{role}**: {content}\n\n"
   - Skip invalid lines with warning comment
   - Used by Claude

3. JSON (.json):
   - Parse as single JSON object
   - Expected schema: { messages: Array<{role, content}> }
   - Format each message as: "**{role}**: {content}\n\n"
   - Handle nested conversation structure
   - Used by various agents

Format Examples:

JSONL Input:
{"role":"user","content":"Hello"}
{"role":"assistant","content":"Hi there!"}

Formatted Output:
**user**: Hello

**assistant**: Hi there!

JSON Input:
{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Hello"},{"role":"assistant","content":"Hi!"}]}

Formatted Output:
**user**: Hello

**assistant**: Hi!

Error Handling:
- Invalid JSON: Include raw line with [PARSE ERROR] prefix
- Missing fields: Use [unknown] for role or [empty] for content
- Unsupported extension: Return raw content with warning header

Key Features:
- Human-readable output
- Preserves conversation structure
- Graceful handling of malformed data
- Consistent formatting across file types

Output:
Returns formatted string ready for LLM extraction.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:33.384464-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:45.417343-05:00"}
